query: Largest IPOs in history first day performance IPO pop percentage
answer:
# Largest IPOs in History: First-Day Performance ("IPO Pop")

## Overview

This report compiles data on the largest IPOs in history by proceeds raised and their first-day trading performance. The "IPO pop" — the percentage gain from the offering price to the first-day closing price — is a key metric for understanding how IPOs are priced relative to market demand. The data covers mega-IPOs across global exchanges and spans from the 1980s to 2024.

---

## Top Largest IPOs by Proceeds and Their First-Day Returns

### The Mega-Deals (>$10B Raised)

| Rank | Company | IPO Date | Exchange | Proceeds (USD Bn) | First-Day Return |
|------|---------|----------|----------|--------------------|------------------|
| 1 | Saudi Aramco | Dec 11, 2019 | Tadawul (Saudi) | ~$25.6B | **+10.0%** |
| 2 | Alibaba Group | Sep 19, 2014 | NYSE | ~$25.0B | **+38.1%** |
| 3 | SoftBank Corp | Dec 19, 2018 | Tokyo SE | ~$23.5B | **-14.5%** |
| 4 | Agricultural Bank of China | Jul 7, 2010 | Shanghai + HKEX | ~$22.1B | **~+0.6% / -2%** |
| 5 | ICBC | Oct 27, 2006 | Shanghai + HKEX | ~$21.9B | **+14.7% (HK)** |
| 6 | AIA Group | Oct 29, 2010 | HKEX | ~$20.5B | **+11.8%** |
| 7 | Visa Inc. | Mar 19, 2008 | NYSE | ~$19.7B | **+28.4%** |
| 8 | General Motors | Nov 18, 2010 | NYSE | ~$18.1B | **+3.6%** |
| 9 | NTT DoCoMo | Oct 22, 1998 | Tokyo SE + NYSE | ~$18.0B | **~+3%** |
| 10 | Enel SpA | Nov 2, 1999 | Borsa Italiana | ~$16.6B | modest gain |
| 11 | Facebook (Meta) | May 18, 2012 | NASDAQ | ~$16.0B | **+0.6%** |
| 12 | NTT (Nippon Telegraph) | Feb 9, 1987 | Tokyo SE | ~$15.3B | **~+33%** |
| 13 | Deutsche Telekom | Nov 18, 1996 | Frankfurt | ~$13.3B | **+18.9%** |
| 14 | Rivian Automotive | Nov 10, 2021 | NASDAQ | ~$11.9B | **+29.1%** |
| 15 | Bank of China | Jun 1, 2006 | HKEX | ~$11.2B | **+15%** |
| 16 | Rosneft | Jul 14, 2006 | London + Moscow | ~$10.7B | **~+0.7%** |
| 17 | AT&T Wireless | Apr 26, 2000 | NYSE | ~$10.6B | **+27%** |
| 18 | Glencore | May 19, 2011 | London + HKEX | ~$10.0B | **-3.2%** |

### Key Finding: Larger IPOs tend to have smaller pops. The average first-day return for the 10 largest IPOs is significantly lower (~8-9%) than for mid-sized IPOs, reflecting more efficient pricing for mega-deals.

---

## Biggest First-Day "Pops" Among Notable IPOs

These are the standout performers in terms of first-day price surge:

| Company | IPO Date | Proceeds | IPO Price | Day-1 Close | **Day-1 Return** |
|---------|----------|----------|-----------|-------------|-----------------|
| Kuaishou Technology | Feb 5, 2021 | ~$5.4B | HK$115 | HK$300 | **+160.9%** |
| Airbnb | Dec 10, 2020 | ~$3.5B | $68.00 | $144.71 | **+112.8%** |
| Snowflake | Sep 16, 2020 | ~$3.4B | $120.00 | $253.93 | **+111.6%** |
| DoorDash | Dec 9, 2020 | ~$3.4B | $102.00 | $189.51 | **+85.8%** |
| Twitter | Nov 7, 2013 | ~$1.8B | $26.00 | $44.90 | **+73%** |
| Astera Labs | Mar 20, 2024 | ~$714M | $36.00 | $65.58 | **+72.2%** |
| Bumble | Feb 11, 2021 | ~$2.2B | $43.00 | $70.31 | **+63.5%** |
| Reddit | Mar 21, 2024 | ~$748M | $34.00 | $50.44 | **+48.4%** |
| Snap Inc. | Mar 2, 2017 | ~$3.4B | $17.00 | $24.48 | **+44%** |
| Coupang | Mar 11, 2021 | ~$4.6B | $35.00 | $49.25 | **+40.7%** |
| Alibaba | Sep 19, 2014 | ~$25.0B | $68.00 | $93.89 | **+38.1%** |

---

## Notable IPO Failures (Negative First-Day Returns)

| Company | IPO Date | Proceeds | **Day-1 Return** |
|---------|----------|----------|-----------------|
| SoftBank Corp | Dec 18, 2018 | ~$23.5B | **-14.5%** |
| Birkenstock | Oct 11, 2023 | ~$1.5B | **-12.6%** |
| Uber Technologies | May 10, 2019 | ~$8.1B | **-7.6%** |
| Glencore | May 19, 2011 | ~$10.0B | **-3.2%** |
| Lineage (REIT) | Jul 25, 2024 | ~$4.4B | **~-2.6%** |

---

## Historical Average IPO First-Day Returns (US Market, Jay Ritter Data)

| Period | Avg. First-Day Return | Market Context |
|--------|----------------------|----------------|
| 1980–1989 | ~7.2% | Post-deregulation era |
| 1990–1998 | ~14.8% | Bull market, tech emergence |
| 1999–2000 | **~65%** | Dot-com bubble peak |
| 2001–2003 | ~11.7% | Post-bubble correction |
| 2004–2007 | ~11.9% | Pre-crisis recovery |
| 2008–2009 | ~9.4% | Global Financial Crisis |
| 2010–2013 | ~13.9% | QE-driven recovery |
| 2014–2016 | ~15.3% | Stable growth |
| 2017–2019 | ~18.2% | Late-cycle tech boom |
| 2020–2021 | **~32%** | ZIRP/SPAC/pandemic boom |
| 2022 | ~17% | Rate hikes, volume collapse |
| 2023 | ~20% | Gradual market reopening |

---

## Recent IPOs (2023–2024)

| Company | IPO Date | Proceeds | Day-1 Return |
|---------|----------|----------|--------------|
| Arm Holdings | Sep 14, 2023 | ~$4.9B | +24.7% |
| Instacart | Sep 19, 2023 | ~$660M | +12.3% |
| Klaviyo | Sep 20, 2023 | ~$576M | +9.2% |
| Birkenstock | Oct 11, 2023 | ~$1.5B | -12.6% |
| Reddit | Mar 21, 2024 | ~$748M | +48.4% |
| Astera Labs | Mar 20, 2024 | ~$714M | +72.2% |
| Viking Holdings | May 1, 2024 | ~$1.5B | +19.0% |
| Ibotta | Apr 18, 2024 | ~$577M | +23.2% |
| Lineage | Jul 25, 2024 | ~$4.4B | ~-2.6% |

---

## Key Analytical Insights

1. **Inverse size-pop relationship**: The very largest IPOs (>$10B) average single-digit first-day returns, while $1-5B IPOs frequently see 20-50%+ pops. Saudi Aramco (+10%), Facebook (+0.6%), and GM (+3.6%) illustrate tight pricing in mega-deals.

2. **"Money left on the table"**: Alibaba's 38% pop on $25B meant ~$9.5B in theoretical value transferred from the company to first-day traders. Academic estimates suggest US IPOs collectively left over **$200 billion** on the table from 1990–2020.

3. **Bubble periods amplify pops dramatically**: The dot-com era (~65% average) and 2020–2021 (~32% average) saw extraordinary underpricing. Kuaishou's +161%, Airbnb's +113%, and Snowflake's +112% are among the most extreme examples for billion-dollar+ IPOs.

4. **Secular trend upward**: Average IPO underpricing has roughly doubled from ~7% in the 1980s to ~15-20% in normal modern markets, driven by changes in allocation practices, increased retail participation, and evolving market structure.

5. **2022 drought, 2023-2024 recovery**: IPO volume collapsed in 2022 due to rising rates. The market cautiously reopened in late 2023 (ARM, Instacart) with mixed results (Birkenstock fell -12.6%). 2024 showed stronger recovery, particularly in tech (Astera Labs +72%, Reddit +48%).

---

**Data Caveats**: Figures are compiled from training knowledge through early 2025. Some exact closing prices may have minor discrepancies depending on the source (e.g., inclusion of greenshoe/overallotment shares in proceeds, or calculation from IPO price vs. opening trade price). For the most authoritative historical dataset, Jay Ritter's IPO data page at the University of Florida is the gold standard. Web-based search tools were unavailable for real-time verification during this research session.

key_facts: Saudi Aramco holds the record for the largest IPO ever at ~$25.6B (Dec 2019), with a +10.0% first-day return
Alibaba's $25.0B IPO (Sep 2014) is the second-largest ever and delivered a +38.1% first-day pop, leaving an estimated $9.5B on the table
SoftBank Corp's $23.5B IPO (Dec 2018) is the largest IPO to decline on day one at -14.5%
Kuaishou Technology had the largest first-day pop among major IPOs at +160.9% on its $5.4B Hong Kong IPO (Feb 2021)
The 2020-2021 period saw historically extreme IPO pops: Airbnb +112.8%, Snowflake +111.6%, DoorDash +85.8%
Average US IPO first-day returns peaked at ~65% during the dot-com bubble (1999-2000) and ~32% during the 2020-2021 boom
There is a well-documented inverse relationship between IPO deal size and first-day return — mega-deals (>$10B) average single-digit pops
Facebook's $16B IPO (May 2012) had one of the flattest first-day returns for a major IPO at just +0.6%
Visa's $19.7B IPO (Mar 2008) popped +28.4% despite occurring during the early Global Financial Crisis
Academic estimates suggest US IPO companies collectively left over $200 billion on the table from 1990-2020 through underpricing
Average IPO first-day returns have roughly doubled from ~7% in the 1980s to ~15-20% in normal modern markets
The 2022 IPO drought saw volume collapse due to rising rates; the market gradually reopened in late 2023 with ARM ($4.9B, +24.7%)
In 2024, notable pops included Astera Labs (+72.2%) and Reddit (+48.4%), signaling recovering IPO appetite
Uber's $8.1B IPO (May 2019) fell -7.6% on day one, making it one of the largest IPOs to decline
Rivian's $11.9B IPO (Nov 2021) popped +29.1% — the largest US IPO of 2021 by proceeds
